# Spec: auto-detect foreign-language fields and emit native TTS

**Outcome**: Vocab decks generated by 2anki speak the word back on the first review in Anki, with no manual setup. Leading indicator: deck downloads that come back for a second conversion within 7 days for language-tagged sources (Quizlet language sets, Notion DB columns named in a non-English language). Target: lift week-2 return rate on language sources by 5 percentage points.

**Goal alignment**: Language learners are the single largest opportunity cluster surfaced in the r/Anki review (50 threads, audio-per-card was the most repeated ask). Anki's native `{{tts lang:Field}}` template syntax costs us nothing to emit — no audio bundled, no API calls, no licensing. Shipping this puts 2anki at parity with the brittle ASBplayer + Yomitan + AnkiConnect stack for the vocab use case and pushes us toward 300K.

**Problem**: A language learner converts a Quizlet set of Spanish vocab or a Notion database with a Japanese column. The deck imports cleanly. They review the first card and there is no audio. To get spoken pronunciation they have to install a TTS add-on, paste each word into Forvo, run a Wiktionary scraper, or record themselves. The native Anki `{{tts es:Spanish}}` template entry would speak the field through the device's built-in voice on Desktop, iOS, and Android — but only if it is present in the template at build time. 2anki already emits this tag for MCQ cards based on a user setting (see `create_deck/helpers/get_model.py:_apply_mcq_settings`); we just do not do it anywhere else.

**Riskiest assumption**: That detection precision on free-text fields is high enough that a default-on toggle does not generate user complaints. False positives (English field tagged as Spanish) are cheap — `{{tts en:Field}}` on an English field still reads correctly — but false confidence (Japanese mis-detected as Chinese) sounds wrong and is the failure mode we cannot ignore.

**Smallest test**: Run the chosen heuristic against a fixture set of 100 fields drawn from existing converted decks (CSV, Notion exports, AI-converted) covering en, es, fr, de, ja, zh, ko. Measure precision per language. If precision on the CJK trio is below 95%, narrow the default-on scope before shipping.

**Scope**:
- **In:** detection of field language during conversion; injection of `{{tts <lang>:<Field>}}` into the front-side template for tagged fields, on any card type (not just MCQ); a single converter toggle "Add speech to language fields"; English as the safe fallback when uncertain.
- **Out:** bundling audio into the `.apkg` (zero infra cost is the point); Forvo / Wiktionary / Anthropic TTS integration; per-card voice selection; speed / pitch UI; per-field override UI; language tag on the card for filtering (deferred to a follow-up).

**User story**: As a language learner converting a Quizlet set or Notion vocab database, I want the resulting Anki deck to read each foreign-language field aloud the first time I review the card, so I learn pronunciation alongside meaning without installing anything.

**What ships**:
1. **Detection.** During conversion, after fields are extracted but before the model template is built, classify each field's aggregated sample text (concat first N values, capped). v1 picks the **CJK Unicode-block heuristic** for ja / zh / ko (cheap, precise, no dependency) and an explicit **source-hint signal** for European languages: Quizlet sets carry a language metadata field; Notion column names matching `/^(spanish|french|german|italian|portuguese|...)/i` get a hint. If no signal, fall back to `en`. `langdetect` is left as an Open Question.
2. **Injection.** Extend `_apply_mcq_settings` in `create_deck/helpers/get_model.py` (or add a sibling `_apply_field_tts` and call it from the same site) so it injects `{{tts <detected-lang>:<Field>}}` into the template for tagged fields on **any** model — not just MCQ. The existing function already proves the build-time injection mechanic works; this widens its scope.
3. **User control.** A single toggle in the converter UI labeled **"Add speech to language fields"**. Default **on** when the source carries an explicit language hint (Quizlet language metadata, Notion column language match). Default **off** when the only signal is a CJK block hit on free text, so a user converting mixed notes does not get surprise audio. State persists with the converter's other settings; no new persistence layer.
4. **Fallback.** When the heuristic returns no confident match, emit `{{tts en:Field}}`. English TTS on an English field is harmless; English TTS on a misdetected field sounds wrong but stays usable.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] Sample CSV with a `Spanish` column produces a deck whose front template contains `{{tts es:Spanish}}`.
- [ ] Sample Notion page with a Japanese vocab column produces `{{tts ja:Word}}`.
- [ ] Imported into Anki Desktop and AnkiMobile, audio plays on first review via the device's native TTS — no add-on, no config.
- [ ] Toggle off suppresses all `{{tts ...}}` injection on non-MCQ models; MCQ behavior is unchanged.
- [ ] Python unit test in `create_deck/helpers/` covers: CJK block detection per script, source-hint match, fallback to `en`, toggle-off path.
- [ ] Browser check on the new converter toggle at 375px per `.claude/rules/browser-attestation.md`.

**Test layers**: Python (`pytest`) for detection and template injection in `create_deck/helpers/`. Server (`jest`) for the converter setting plumbed through `routes` → `controllers` → `usecases` → `services` → `create_deck` invocation. Web (`vitest`) for the toggle component.

**Open questions**:
- **Heuristic choice for v1.** CJK-only (precise, narrow coverage) versus adding the `langdetect` Python library (broad coverage, ~1 MB dep, statistical so produces some misclassification on short strings). Recommendation: ship v1 with CJK + source-hint only; revisit `langdetect` after measuring precision on the fixture set.
- **Surface the detected language as a card tag** (e.g. `lang::ja`) so users can filter? Useful but adds tag surface area. Defer to v2 unless engineering finds it trivial during implementation.
- **Notion source-hint mapping.** Does the Notion database column language come through the existing extraction layer, or does this require touching `services/NotionService/`? If the latter, scope grows from S to M.

**Out of scope (next iteration)**:
- Audio bundling via Forvo, Wiktionary, or a TTS API (cost, licensing, storage — explicitly rejected).
- Voice / speed / pitch selection UI.
- Per-card or per-field language override.
- Language tag emission for filtering.
- AI-converted card path beyond the toggle defaulting to off there (revisit once Claude can label field language as part of its output).
